_meta.description: Wrong-doc lock policy enforcing explicit filename/doc lock constraints and discovery exceptions.

rules.trigger: query contains an explicit filename and chosen document does not match
examples: User asks about 'invoice_2025.pdf' but answer uses 'invoice_2024.pdf'.
'Show contract_A.docx' but retrieval returned contract_B.docx.

rules.trigger: state.activeDocRef has silently changed to a different document
rules.check: context.state.activeDocRef.present == true AND context.state.activeDocRef.matchesExplicit == false AND context.explicitDocRef.present == true
examples: Active ref drifted from budget.xlsx to forecast.xlsx.
User set docRef to A, system switched to B.

rules.trigger: explicit doc ref active and source buttons contain non-matching documents
examples: User locked to report.pdf, pills show budget.xlsx too.
Source buttons include 3 docs when only 1 is referenced.

rules.trigger: retrieval results contain passages from documents other than the explicitly referenced one
rules.check: context.explicitDocRef.present == true AND context.retrievedPassages.any(p => p.docId != context.explicitDocRef.id)
examples: Retrieval returns 5 passages, 2 from wrong doc.
Cross-doc contamination in retrieval set.

rules.trigger: answer contains factual data that only exists in a non-referenced document
rules.check: context.explicitDocRef.present == true AND output.facts.any(f => f.sourceDocId != context.explicitDocRef.id)
examples: Revenue figure from budget.xlsx appears in answer about invoice.pdf.
Clause from contract B in answer about contract A.

rules.trigger: user mentions a specific filename in the query and no doc lock is active
examples: User says 'in report.pdf' — should activate doc lock.
'Look at budget.xlsx' — needs lock.

rules.trigger: follow-up query doesn't mention a filename but previous turn had an explicit doc lock
rules.check: context.docLock.active == true AND context.currentQuery.filenameRef == null AND context.conversationContinued == true
examples: T1: 'What's in report.pdf?' T2: 'What about page 3?' — lock should persist.
Follow-up pronoun should inherit lock.

rules.trigger: query explicitly references a different document than the current lock
rules.check: context.docLock.active == true AND context.queryFilenameRef.present == true AND context.queryFilenameRef.id != context.docLock.docId
examples: Locked to A.pdf, user now asks about B.xlsx.
Explicit new doc ref should switch the lock.

rules.trigger: query is explicitly cross-document and current doc lock should be released
examples: 'Compare all reports' should release single-doc lock.
'Across all documents' signals broad scope.

rules.trigger: high-confidence wrong-doc detection — answer is about an entirely different document
examples: User asks about tax return, answer discusses employment contract.
Asked about Q3 report, answer from Q1 report.

rules.trigger: multiple documents have similar filenames and query could match any
examples: 'report.pdf' and 'report_v2.pdf' both exist.
'budget_2024.xlsx' and 'budget_2025.xlsx'.

rules.trigger: query asks about a specific document type but answer uses a different type
examples: Asked about 'the spreadsheet' but answer cites a PDF.
'The contract' query answered with data from an invoice.

rules.trigger: answer synthesizes from multiple documents without explicit user permission
rules.check: output.sourceDocs.count > 1 AND context.crossDocPermission == false AND context.explicitDocRef.present == true
examples: User asked about one doc, answer blends data from 3.
Cross-doc synthesis without disclosure.

rules.trigger: user references a document name that doesn't exist in the uploaded corpus
examples: User asks about 'report_2030.pdf' which doesn't exist.
Filename typo — no match in corpus.

rules.trigger: query uses a partial filename that matches multiple documents
examples: 'The report' matches 'annual_report.pdf' and 'quarterly_report.pdf'.
'Budget' matches 3 budget files.

rules.trigger: documents have version suffixes and query doesn't specify which version
examples: 'contract_v1.pdf', 'contract_v2.pdf', 'contract_final.pdf'.
'draft' vs 'final' version.

rules.trigger: documents have date-based names and query doesn't specify the date
examples: 'report_jan.pdf' and 'report_feb.pdf'.
'invoice_2024_01' through 'invoice_2024_12'.

rules.trigger: editing mode is active and edit targets a different document than the active one
examples: User is editing contract A, edit applies to contract B.
Edit operation crosses document boundary.

rules.trigger: user has a document open in a tab and query references a different document
rules.check: context.openTab.docId != null AND context.queryDocRef != context.openTab.docId AND context.queryDocRef != null
examples: Tab shows invoice.pdf, user asks about 'the contract'.
Open doc differs from referenced doc.

rules.trigger: nav_pills mode should always search across all documents — no doc lock in nav mode
examples: Nav search should span all docs.
Doc lock should not restrict nav discovery.

rules.trigger: query explicitly asks to list all documents — discovery exception
examples: 'Show me all my documents' should override lock.
'List uploaded files' is a discovery query.

rules.trigger: query uses 'across all' or 'in any document' phrasing — discovery exception
examples: 'Find this term across all documents.'
'In any of my files, where does it say...?'

rules.trigger: user references a specific sheet tab in an xlsx and answer uses data from a different sheet
examples: User asks about 'Sheet2' but answer uses 'Sheet1' data.
Tab 'Revenue' referenced, 'Expenses' tab used.

rules.trigger: user references a specific slide number in a pptx and answer uses a different slide
examples: Asked about slide 5, answer from slide 12.
Specific slide referenced but data from another.

rules.trigger: user uses a nickname or alias for a document and system resolves to wrong doc
examples: 'The quarterly' resolves to annual report.
'NDA' resolves to wrong non-disclosure agreement.

rules.trigger: doc lock references a document that has been deleted or replaced
examples: Locked to deleted file.
Document was re-uploaded with different ID.

rules.trigger: response includes an attachment (table, chart) from a document that is not the locked doc
rules.check: context.docLock.active == true AND output.attachments.any(a => a.sourceDocId != context.docLock.docId)
examples: Table from wrong spreadsheet in response.
Chart pulled from unlocked document.

rules.trigger: two edit operations target different documents in the same turn
examples: Single turn tries to edit both invoice.docx and report.docx.
Cross-doc edit in one request.

rules.trigger: no explicit doc ref but conversation context strongly implies a specific document
examples: Discussing contract details for 3 turns — implied lock.
Follow-up clearly about the same doc.

rules.trigger: summary or comparison includes data points from a document the user didn't ask about
examples: Summary includes unrequested document.
Comparison adds a third doc not asked for.

rules.trigger: doc lock state changed but no audit event was emitted
examples: Lock activated without telemetry.
Lock released silently.

rules.trigger: answer references documents from a different workspace than the user's current one
examples: Cross-workspace doc leak.
Data from team B's workspace in team A's answer.

rules.trigger: user scope is explicitly limited to attached documents only
rules.check: context.attachedDocsOnly == true AND output.sourceDocs.any(d => context.attachedDocIds.includes(d) == false)
examples: User asks 'use only attached docs' but answer cites indexed corpus docs.
Attached-only request includes a source from a non-attached file.

rules.trigger: query intent is compare and fewer than two explicit documents are resolved
examples: User says 'compare these' but only one document is explicitly identified.
Comparison requested without two explicit docs in scope.

finance: I found multiple financial documents matching '{{queryRef}}'. Which one do you mean: {{candidateList}}?
Your question references '{{docRef}}' but I can only find '{{closestMatch}}'. Should I use that?
I'm currently focused on {{lockedDoc}}. Would you like me to look at {{otherDoc}} instead?
Multiple budget files exist: {{fileList}}. Which fiscal period?
The figure you're asking about appears in both {{docA}} and {{docB}} — which is authoritative?
legal: I see {{count}} versions of '{{contractName}}'. Which version: {{versionList}}?
Amendment or original? Both contain clause {{clauseRef}}.
This clause appears in {{docA}} and {{docB}}. Which contract governs?
Document '{{docRef}}' was not found. Did you mean '{{suggestion}}'?
I'm locked to {{currentDoc}}. The term you mentioned is in {{otherDoc}} — switch?
medical: Multiple patient records match. Which encounter: {{encounterList}}?
Lab results exist in {{docA}} and {{docB}}. Which panel date: {{dateList}}?
I'm focused on {{currentDoc}}. Switch to {{otherDoc}} for {{dataType}}?
Prescription record '{{rxRef}}' not found — closest match is '{{suggestion}}'.
ops: Data exists in {{sheetA}} and {{sheetB}}. Which sheet?
Inventory data appears in {{count}} documents. Which warehouse/location?
Report '{{docRef}}' has {{versionCount}} versions. Date range or version number?
